# @andes/dropdown

A dropdown allows you to select one of several mutually exclusive options. It can be used in forms or standalone.

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Dropdown](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/843296-dropdown/b/26f535)_.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/dropdown
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the components in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/dropdown/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```scss
// Base or standalone
@import '@andes/dropdown/base';

// Form
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/form';
@import '@andes/badge/index';
@import '@andes/form/lib/styles/helper/index';

// Multiple
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/multiple';
@import '@andes/checkbox/index';
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/lib/styles/multiple';
@import '@andes/tag/base';
@import '@andes/tag/lib/styles/tagCollapsed';

// Native
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/native';
@import '@andes/badge/index';
@import '@andes/form/lib/styles/helper/index';

// Standalone size="small"
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/standalone/size/small';

// Standalone size="medium"
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/standalone/size/medium';

// Search
@import '@andes/form/index';
@import '@andes/search-box/index';
```

> Always import `@andes/dropdown/base` and then only the styles that you need.

> :warning: Due to Checkbox and List variables being used in FloatingMenu multiple styles, you need to import them beforehand

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Dropdown, DropdownItem, DropdownGroup, DropdownNative, DropdownNativeItem } from '@andes/dropdown';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Dropdown](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/dropdown-new--andes-dropdown-form-single).

### Standalone variant

This variant is a dropdown menu list.

```jsx
<DropdownStandalone
  id="component-id"
  label="Ordenar por"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownItem value="min-price" title="Menor precio" />
  <DropdownItem value="max-price" title="Mayor precio" />
  <DropdownItem value="featured" title="Más relevantes" />
</DropdownStandalone>
```

### Form variant

This variant is used in forms. The trigger looks like a form control (like TextField), and renders a custon select dropdown.

```jsx
<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="País"
  placeholder="Selecciona tu país de residencia"
  helper="Helper text"
  defaultValue="argentina"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownItem value="argentina" title="Argentina" />
  <DropdownItem value="brazil" title="Brazil" />
  <DropdownItem value="uruguay" title="Uruguay" />
  <DropdownItem value="chile" title="Chile" />
</Dropdown>
```

It also accepts grouped options:

```jsx
<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="Médios de pago"
  placeholder="Seleccionar"
  helper="Selecciona la forma más conveniente para pagar el producto"
  defaultValue="visa"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownGroup label="Tarjetas de crédito">
    <DropdownItem value="visa" title="Visa" />
    <DropdownItem value="mastercard" title="Mastercard" />
    <DropdownItem value="amex" title="American Express" />
  </DropdownGroup>
  <DropdownGroup label="Efectivo en redes de pago">
    <DropdownItem value="rapipado" title="RapiPago" />
    <DropdownItem value="pagofacil" title="PagoFacil" />
  </DropdownGroup>
</Dropdown>
```

Also, you will be able to render a multi-selection dropdown

```jsx
<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="Operación"
  placeholder="Selecciona el típo de operación que busca"
  helper="Selecciona el típo de operación que busca"
  defaultValue="rent"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
  multiple
>
  <DropdownItem value="rental" title="Alquiler" />
  <DropdownItem value="sell" title="Venta" />
  <DropdownItem value="temporary-rental" title="Alquiler temporal" />
</Dropdown>
```

### Native

Native is used in forms, recommended to use on **WebMobile** for accessibility matters. The trigger looks like a form control (like TextField), and renders a native select dropdown.

```jsx
<DropdownNative
  id="component-id"
  label="País"
  helper="Seleccione un país"
  onChange={this.onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownNativeItem value="argentina" title="Argentina" />
  <DropdownNativeItem value="brazil" title="Brazil" />
  <DropdownNativeItem value="uruguay" title="Uruguay" />
</DropdownNative>
```

### Uncontrolled mode

Use this mode if you only want to consume the current selected value of the dropdown

```jsx
const onChangeHandler = (event, value) => {
  const selectedOption = value
  ...
}

<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="País"
  placeholder="Selecciona tu país de residencia"
  helper="Helper text"
  defaultValue="argentina"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownItem value="argentina" title="Argentina" />
  <DropdownItem value="brazil" title="Brazil" />
  <DropdownItem value="uruguay" title="Uruguay" />
  <DropdownItem value="chile" title="Chile" />
</Dropdown>
```

### Controlled mode

Use this mode if you want not only to consume the current selected value of the dropdown, but also change it programmatically in runtime.

```jsx
const [value, setValue] = useState('argentina');

const onChangeHandler = (event, selectedOption) => {
  setValue(selectedOption)
}

<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="País"
  placeholder="Selecciona tu país de residencia"
  helper="Helper text"
  value={value}
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownItem value="argentina" title="Argentina" />
  <DropdownItem value="brazil" title="Brazil" />
  <DropdownItem value="uruguay" title="Uruguay" />
  <DropdownItem value="chile" title="Chile" />
</Dropdown>
```

> [!NOTE]  
> We strongly recommend to use the second parameter to access to the dropdown's selected options.
>
> The `event.target.value` might have differences with the real selected option.

### Shadow DOM

Usage inside an isolated component with Shadow DOM. See [@andes/shadow-dom](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/blob/master/packages/shadow-dom/README.md) package

```jsx
import { useEnableAndesShadowDOM } from '@andes/shadow-dom';

useEnableAndesShadowDOM();

<Dropdown
  id="component-id"
  label="País"
  placeholder="Selecciona tu país de residencia"
  helper="Helper text"
  defaultValue="argentina"
  onChange={onChangeHandler}
>
  <DropdownItem value="argentina" title="Argentina" />
  <DropdownItem value="brazil" title="Brazil" />
  <DropdownItem value="uruguay" title="Uruguay" />
  <DropdownItem value="chile" title="Chile" />
</Dropdown>
```

## Props

### `<Dropdown>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `allowUnselect` | Whether the dropdown should allow unselect the current selected option. This is not valid for `multiple`. | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `children` | Custom content, should be one or a collection or a mix of `<DropdownGroup>` and `<DropdownItem>` | `element` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultOpen` | Whether the dropdown should be open or not by default. (uncontrolled mode) | `boolean` | `undefined` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Default selected value for dropdown (uncontrolled mode) | `string \| Array<string>` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the dropdown should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `helper` | Text to display as a helper, hint or error to the user. Only available for `type: 'form'` | `string` | `''` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `label` | Text to display as label. In `type: 'standalone'` it will act as a fallback placeholder too | `string` | `''` |  |
| `menuAlignment` | <sup>1</sup>Dropdown menu alignment. When `multiple` or `search` is on, it always be `'bottom'` | `string: 'center' \| 'bottom'` | `'center'` |  |
| `menuMaxHeight` | Dropdown menu max height.  It can be used for large options amount | `number` | `undefined` |  |
| `modifier` | Component modifier. Readonly is not currently implemented. Only available for `type: 'form'` | `string:  error \| readonly`| `undefined` |  |
| `multiple` | Allows a multiple selection menu | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `onChange` | Callback executed when an option is selected. When `multiple` is on, values will be an array | `function(event, values)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClose` | Callback executed when the dropdown menu is closed | `function()` | `undefined` |  |
| `onOpen` | Callback executed when the dropdown menu is open | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `open` | Whether the dropdown should be open or not (controlled mode) | `boolean` | `undefined` |  |
| `placeholder` | Text to display as placeholder at the initial state of Dropdown. Only available for `type: 'form'` | `string` | `''` |  |
| `renderCustomTrigger` | <sup>2</sup>Callback to render a custom content inside the trigger button. It should return an element to render | `function(actualContent, currentValues)` | `undefined` | |
| `renderMultipleTriggerContent` | Callback to render custom content within the trigger when the dropdown is set to multiple. It should return an element to render. | `function(values, Object<MultipleContentRenderProp>)` | `undefined` | |
| `search` | Whether the dropdown should show a searchbox to filter dropdown items | `bool, Object<SearchConfig>` | `false` |  |
| `size` | Defines the sizing of the dropdown trigger. Only available for `DropdownStandalone` | `string:  'small' \| 'medium' \| 'large'` | `'large'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Text label for screen readers | `string` | `''` |  |
| `value` | Current selected value for dropdown (controlled mode) | `string \| Array<string>` | `undefined` |  |

#### SearchConfig

```jsx
  {
    defaultValue: string, // default: undefined
    filterCondition: function(searchText, value, dropdownItem), // default: undefined
    onChange: function(event), // default: undefined
    placeholder: string, // default: undefined
  }
```

#### MultipleContentRenderProp

```jsx
  {
    getTagCollapsedProps: function(displayValues),
    getTagProps: function(displayValue, index),
  }
```

> 1 - `menuAlignment` **center** is not recommended to use along with `menuMaxHeight`. Scrollable and centered menus may cause issues in some browsers.

> 2 - If using slots (`children`) in `DropdownItem`, note that the first parameter passed to this callback will always be a string representing the item's value.

---

### `<DropdownItem>` extends `<FloatingMenuItem>` [API](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/tree/master/packages/floating-menu#floatingmenuitem-extends-listitem-api)

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content. Should only be used in specific cases where usage of `primary` and `secondary` props are not enough. (not recommended) | node |  |  |
| `description` | Text to display as secondary title | `string` | `''` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the item should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `rightContent` | Text to display as tertiary wording | `string` | `''` |  |
| `title` | Text to display as primary title | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `value` | The value of the item that will be passed when is selected.  | `string` | | ✅|

---

### `<DropdownGroup>` extends `<FloatingMenuGroup>` [API](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/tree/master/packages/floating-menu#floatingmenugroup-extends-listgroup-api)

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content, should be one or a collection of `<DropdownItem>` | `node` | |✅ |

---

### `<DropdownNative>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content, should be one or a collection of `<DropdownNativeItem>` | `element` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Text or number marked as default selected value for dropdown (uncontrolled behavior) | `string\| number` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the dropdown should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `helper` | Text to display as a helper, hint or error to the user | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `label` | Label associated to the select | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `modifier` | Component modifier. Readonly is not currently implemented | `string:  error \| readonly`| `undefined` |  |
| `onChange` | Action that performs when one item is selected | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `value` | Text or number marked as selected value for dropdown (controlled behavior) | `string \| number` | `undefined` |  |

### `<DropdownNativeItem>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `disabled` | Whether the item should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `title` | Text to display as primary title | `string` | `''` | ✅ |
| `selected` | Whether the item should be selected | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `value` | The value of the item that will be passed when is selected | `string \| number` | `null` | |

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

Dropdown allows you to modify the following attributes.

| Sub Attribute | Description                 |
| ------------- | --------------------------- |
| `fill`        | Dropdown fill color.        |
| `border`      | Dropdown border color.      |
| `label`       | Dropdown label color.       |
| `placeholder` | Dropdown placeholder color. |
| `value`       | Dropdown value color.       |
| `chevron`     | Dropdown chevron color.     |
| `icon`        | Dropdown icon color.        |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    dropdown: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          [state]: fillColorValue,
        },
        label: {
          [state]: textColorValue,
        },
        placeholder: {
          [state]: textColorValue,
        },
        chevron: {
          [state]: iconColorValue,
        },
        border: {
          [state]: borderColorValue,
        },
        value: {
          [state]: textColorValue,
        },
        icon: {
          default: iconColorValue,
          disabled: iconColorValue,
        },
      },
      standalone: {
        color: {
          chevron: {
            [state]: iconColorValue,
          },
          value: {
            [state]: textColorValue,
          },
          icon: {
            default: iconColorValue,
            disabled: iconColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

The possible case values ​​are:

```jsx

state: default | hover | active | disabled | error | readOnly;

```

> More states may be added in the future, so we recommend consulting the @andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json file for more detailed information

### Example

<img src="resources/dropdown/docs/Dropdown-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Dropdown Example" width="320" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { DropdownStandalone, DropdownItem } from '@andes/dropdown';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    dropdown: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          default: '#fff',
        },
        label: {
          default: '#101014',
        },
        placeholder: {
          default: '#736f8c',
        },
        chevron: {
          default: '#736f8c',
        },
        border: {
          default: '#d9d9d9',
          active: '#6f42e3',
        },
        value: {
          default: '#736f8c',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <DropdownStandalone label="Label" placeholder="Selected option">
      <DropdownItem title="Item 1" value="1" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 2" value="2" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 3" value="3" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 4" value="4" />
    </DropdownStandalone>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/dropdown/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    dropdown: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes dropdown regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

>[!WARNING]
> In this component the CSS styles will be applied to the container of the dropdown, since it has a composed layout CSS properties functionality may not work for your use case.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                       | Description                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `data-andes-dropdown`                | Dropdown container tag.                     |
| `data-andes-dropdown-type`           | Dropdown container type tag. Can be: `'form'`, `'native'` or `'standalone'`. |
| `data-andes-state`                   | Dropdown container state tag. Can be: `'disabled'`, `'readonly'` or `'error'`. |
| `data-andes-dropdown-label`          | Dropdown label tag.                         |
| `data-andes-dropdown-trigger-wrapper`| Dropdown trigger wrapper tag.               |
| `data-andes-dropdown-trigger-content`| Dropdown trigger content tag.               |
| `data-andes-dropdown-value`          | Dropdown trigger value tag.                 |
| `data-andes-dropdown-placeholder`    | Dropdown trigger placeholder tag.           |
| `data-andes-dropdown-chevron`        | Dropdown trigger chevron tag.               |
| `data-andes-dropdown-helper`         | Dropdown helper tag.                        |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

### Example

<img src="resources/dropdown/docs/Dropdown-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Dropdown Example" width="320" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { DropdownStandalone, DropdownItem } from '@andes/dropdown';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    dropdown: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '[data-andes-dropdown-label]': {
          background: '#fff',
          color: '#686868',
          marginBottom: '-9px',
          marginLeft: '6px',
          padding: '0 2px',
          position: 'relative',
          width: 'max-content',
          zIndex: 1,
        },
        '[data-andes-dropdown] > div': {
          border: '2px solid #b1b1b1',
          borderRadius: '3px',
        },
        '[data-andes-dropdown-trigger]': {
          outline: 'none',
          borderRadius: '3px',
        },
        '[data-andes-dropdown-chevron]': {
          color: '#686868',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <DropdownStandalone label="Label" placeholder="Selected option">
      <DropdownItem title="Item 1" value="1" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 2" value="2" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 3" value="3" />
      <DropdownItem title="Item 4" value="4" />
    </DropdownStandalone>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/dropdown/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
